summary: Cycle H (8th overnight-triage fire of UTC 2026-05-23, ~55min after cycle G — sustained ~1h cadence consistent with F→G ~56min, refuting cycle F's throttled-cron hypothesis across 2 consecutive cycles after the E→F outlier) ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 29th consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED, round-trip 0 failures sustained (23s elapsed at 11:01:02Z; heartbeat at 11:01:21Z status=PASS). Three substantive findings beyond pure heartbeat: (1) GATE-FAIL semantics 3rd-observation corroboration — cycle H found heartbeat status=GATE-FAIL at cycle open (11:00:33Z) written by post-commit-hook at 10:07:46Z for HEAD ce095dfd (cycle G's commit). This is positive direct evidence captured verbatim, confirming cycle F's 2-observation pattern (ca542750 Founder Maintenance + f5a3168b cycle F's own commit). (2) Routine-cron negative case confirmed — e4db2359 (post-cycle-G routine cron, AMD-019/020 auto-clean) landed between cycle G's commit and cycle H open, but the heartbeat at cycle H open still showed head_sha=ce095dfd from 10:07:46Z, NOT head_sha=e4db2359. This means e4db2359's post-commit hook did NOT touch the heartbeat, resolving cycle G's three-reconciliation ambiguity in favor of interpretation (c): the routine cron post-commit hook is a different code path that doesn't run regen-all and doesn't write the heartbeat. (3) Cadence natural-variability sustained — G→H ~55min + F→G ~56min = 2 consecutive ~1h cycles after the E→F ~5h outlier, well-supporting the natural-variability interpretation. Founder G→H window quiet (0 substantive + 1 routine cron e4db2359), consistent with sleep continuation. Three pre-existing untracked Founder files unchanged from cycle G close. Single yellow user-context-gate continues (main-flows.html ~8.37d stale, was 8.33d at G — natural drift). Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST — empty-inbox honestly recorded; GATE-FAIL 3rd-observation is positive direct evidence captured verbatim; routine-cron negative case is direct evidence from heartbeat state read at cycle open. Seven carry-over Founder action items preserved unchanged from cycle G close (no new items, no drops; cycle H sharpens evidence on items #1 cadence-sustained and #7 GATE-FAIL 3rd-observation + routine-cron negative case).
founder_action_items: Cron cadence - now 13th consecutive flag. Cycle H's G→H gap (~55min) sustains the natural-variability interpretation across 2 consecutive ~1h cycles after the E→F ~5h outlier. The probability that E→F was a single outlier event (not a cadence change) is now well-supported by positive evidence. Original Founder-action remains: edit overnight-triage cron prompt to add Step-0 guard that exits clean if `git log --since='3h'` shows no inbox motion AND last regen-all heartbeat is < 60 min old. Founder may also consider that 29 consecutive empty-inbox cycles is itself a signal worth a Step-0 guard regardless of cadence variability.
A8_performance: Lighthouse 65/100 on 1 page (target 75+) - open .lighthouseci/*.html, fix top failures (carry-over from cycles S→G; dimension score holds due to rolling-window mechanics).

main-flows.html user-context capture ~8.37 days stale at cycle H (was 8.33 at cycle G — natural drift over ~55min) - run `node scripts/visual-audit/founder-context-capture.mjs` before next ship-close (not blocking; same diagnosis as cycles S→G).
quota-status weekly_cap field still null - no % computation possible (preserved Founder-triage item from cycles R→G; sidecar discipline OK per [quota-status-schema] check).

aggregate-fiq-status D40 parity GATE-FAIL - stale-timestamp >24h hard threshold tripped because FIQ has had zero churn across 29 consecutive empty-inbox cycles (~29h+ elapsed). NOT an aggregator bug. Will trip on every cycle until FIQ activity resumes or remedy lands (4 remedies enumerated in cycle B post-commit observation).

A12_operational - sustained yellow as of cycle G (continued oscillation across rolling-window mechanics). Cycle H ran clean; current dimension score visible in cycle H's regenerated docs/reports/app-health.html.

Heartbeat `status: GATE-FAIL` field semantics - NOW 3-OBSERVATION-CONFIRMED + ROUTINE-CRON NEGATIVE-CASE CONFIRMED. Cycle H found heartbeat status=GATE-FAIL at cycle open (11:00:33Z) written by post-commit-hook at 10:07:46Z for HEAD ce095dfd (cycle G's commit). Combined with cycle F's two observations (ca542750 + f5a3168b), the pattern is now 3-observation-confirmed: post-commit-hook for `Overnight triage ...` commits writes status=GATE-FAIL within a few minutes. Critically, between cycle G's commit and cycle H open, one routine cron commit (e4db2359 AMD-019/020 auto-clean) landed — but the heartbeat at cycle H open still showed head_sha=ce095dfd from 10:07:46Z, NOT head_sha=e4db2359. This proves the routine cron post-commit hook is a different code path that doesn't write the heartbeat (interpretation (c) from cycle G's three-reconciliation enumeration is now positively confirmed). Tightened Founder-action recommendation: inspect `.husky/post-commit` to find (1) what `Overnight triage ...` commits trigger that AMD-019/020 auto-clean does NOT, and (2) why that trigger writes status=GATE-FAIL instead of PASS. Cycle H stops short of inspecting the hook source — that crosses into 'investigate, propose fix' territory which is a Founder-decision boundary for governance change.
